what_it_does: Implements semantic recall over a session transcript: chunks genuine content turns at multiple scales, embeds them through a served perplexity embedder, persists an index with a freshness sidecar, and answers natural-language queries by cosine retrieval followed by an optional served jina-v3 reranker.
observations: Consumes a served embedder at :8007 and a served reranker at :8008 rather than loading models locally.
Chunks are produced by runtime/session_scan and further split into dimension/section/turn scales with scale and parent metadata.
Uses documents-mode embedding with late chunking, grouping chunks into context-sized documents.
Stores vectors in a .recall.jsonl file and freshness metadata in a .recall.meta.json sidecar keyed on source bytes and mtime.
Implements auto-rebuild when the live source has grown, and declares staleness when auto-rebuild is disabled.
Defines a RecallError fail-loud exception for unreachable embedder or index mismatch.
Provides a CLI wrapper and many environment-variable knobs for URLs, models, budgets, chunk modes, and timeouts.
Strips the harness system-reminder marker before embedding.
standouts: Explicit seam comment: this module CONSUMES the lead's served models and loads none itself.
Multi-scale chunk taxonomy (dimension / section / turn) with dedup logic so coarser scales are emitted only when they genuinely group finer ones.
Index freshness detection via source byte/mtime stamps, with declared staleness instead of silent stale serving.
Fail-loud teaching error when the embedder is down, with a concrete remediation command.
conventions: Heavy docstrings with design rationale and references to decisions/people.
Environment-variable driven configuration with sensible defaults.
Fail-loud error handling; no silent fallbacks.
Direct urllib.request POSTs to local HTTP services.
concerns: _embed_chunks truncates each text to GROUP_CHAR_BUDGET for the request but accumulates cur_chars from the untruncated length, so a single oversized chunk can still exceed the intended group budget.
_embed_documents assumes the endpoint returns vectors in the exact flat order matching the input groups without validating returned indices or dimensions.
The freshness stamp is captured before embedding, so a concurrent live write can leave the index slightly stale without being detected until the next query.
The rerank path truncates candidate text to ~600 characters, which may drop critical signal from long dimensions.
No actual concurrency is implemented despite the batching comment; groups are sent in one request per batch of up to 8.
notes: This is the semantic-memory layer for session transcripts: chunk, embed, index, freshness-check, retrieve, rerank — all via served endpoints so it stays GPU/CPU-bridge-free.
questions: What exact schema does runtime/session_scan return, especially the boundary_point and is_boundary fields?
What response shape does the :8007 embedder return, and does it guarantee order preservation for documents-mode batches?
How is the lead's embedder/reranker started and kept alive?
What is the intended behavior when a query is shorter than a dimension chunk but the answer spans multiple dimensions?
How should the system handle int8/unnormalized vectors if the served endpoint returns them as integers rather than floats?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it builds semantic recall over a session by embedding turns and queries in the same space, ranking by cosine, and reranking.
apparent_themes: Separation of model serving from model consumption
intention_signals: The file is trying to make an agent remember what was decided in the current conversation by turning transcript turns into searchable embeddings, while avoiding the silent failures that broke the earlier embedding-decision recall.
novelty: The combination of a served-embedder-only seam, multi-scale chunk taxonomy with dedup, and a freshness sidecar that detects a growing live session source.
contribution: A self-contained semantic recall pipeline that chunks session content at multiple scales, embeds via a served endpoint, persists vectors with freshness metadata, and retrieves plus reranks natural-language answers over the current session.
summary_for_embedding: runtime/session_recall.py is a semantic recall module that embeds session transcript chunks and queries in the same vector space, indexes them with a freshness sidecar, and returns ranked hits via cosine similarity and an optional served reranker.
intention_for_embedding: It was built so an agent can answer 'what did we decide about X in this session?' by searching embedded conversation history instead of relying on raw transcript scanning.

symbols.does: Fail-loud exception for recall failures such as a down embedder or index/embedding mismatch.
symbols.description: A thin RuntimeError subclass used to make every recall failure explicit; the module refuses to return silent empty results or fall back quietly.

symbols.does: Splits a long text into overlapping size-bounded windows, preferring sentence or newline boundaries.
symbols.description: Fallback chunker used inside over-large structural units; it walks the text with a fixed window and overlap, cutting at the nearest prior sentence or newline to keep fragments readable.

symbols.does: Dimension-aware chunking that splits on headers, paragraphs, and list items before size-windowing.
symbols.description: The default chunker preserves the internal structure of dense messages by cutting at markdown headers, blank-line paragraphs, and bullet/numbered/lettered items, then merging tiny adjacent fragments up to a minimum size.

symbols.does: Coarse section-level chunking that groups paragraphs up to a larger budget.
symbols.description: Produces one rung coarser chunks than dimensions by grouping header/paragraph-delimited paragraphs until a section character budget is reached, enabling broader-context recall matches.

symbols.does: Turns a session JSONL into multi-scale recall chunks with attribution and scale metadata.
symbols.description: Reads genuine content turns from the scanner, extracts their text, and emits dimension chunks plus optional section and turn chunks, ensuring coarser scales are only emitted when they group multiple finer ones.

symbols.does: Loads raw message text for a set of line numbers from the JSONL.
symbols.description: Reuses the scanner's tolerant reader to fetch content strings for the selected turn lines, stripping the harness system-reminder marker before embedding.

symbols.does: POSTs groups of text chunks to the served embedder in documents mode.
symbols.description: Sends a JSON body with the configured model and documents list, expects per-chunk contextual vectors in return, and reshapes the flat response back into the original group structure; raises RecallError on any failure.

symbols.does: Batches many chunk texts into context-sized document groups and embeds them.
symbols.description: Groups texts by turn count and character budget, then calls _embed_documents in batches of up to eight groups, flattening the returned vectors into a single list aligned with the input chunks.

symbols.does: Embeds a single query string through the served embedder.
symbols.description: Wraps the query as a one-document group and returns the first vector, ensuring the query lives in the same embedding space as the index.

symbols.does: POSTs query and candidate texts to the served jina-v3 reranker.
symbols.description: Calls the local CPU reranker service and returns its ranking list, keeping the rerank step out-of-process and free of local torch dependencies.

symbols.does: Computes cosine similarity between two vectors.
symbols.description: Simple dot-product over L2-normalized vectors; returns 0.0 for zero-length vectors.

symbols.does: Determines output directory and file paths for the vector index and metadata sidecar.
symbols.description: Defaults to the source directory and uses the JSONL basename to name .recall.jsonl and .recall.meta.json files.

symbols.does: Records the source file's byte size and modification time.
symbols.description: Used as a cheap freshness fingerprint so later recall calls can detect whether the live session transcript has grown since the index was built.

symbols.does: Compares the current source stamp against the index metadata to report freshness.
symbols.description: Returns whether the index exists, whether it matches the current source bytes and mtime, and a human-readable reason if it is stale or unreadable.

symbols.does: Chunks and embeds a session, then persists vectors and freshness metadata.
symbols.description: Produces multi-scale chunks, embeds them, validates vector count alignment, writes the .recall.jsonl vector file and .recall.meta.json sidecar, and returns the resulting paths and chunk count.

symbols.does: Loads the persisted vector index from JSONL into memory.
symbols.description: Reads each non-empty line of the .recall.jsonl file and parses it as JSON, returning a list of chunk records that include their embedding vectors.

symbols.does: Answers a query by embedding it, retrieving top cosine hits, and optionally reranking them.
symbols.description: Ensures the index exists and is fresh, embeds the query, scores all indexed chunks by cosine similarity, sends a capped pool to the served reranker if enabled, and returns the top k hits with scores, scale, and source attribution.
